# IrisWithSecureModule

An interface module for WithSecure Elements (EDR) and DFIR-IRIS designed to automatically add IOCs and Assets with a 'high' or 'critical' severity status once a BCD is triggered.  
**Module type**: `Processor`  
**Min IRIS version required**: `> 1.4.0` 

## Configuration 
Four parameters must be configured before the module can be enabled.
First, generate your API key from your WithSecure Elements portal, then configure the following settings:

* `WithSecure API Key ClientID`: Must contain the **ClientID** part of the API key generated by WithSecure.
* `WithSecure Api Secret`: Must contain the **Secret** part of the API key generated by WithSecure.
* `FQDN name of DFIR-IRIS`: Must contain the FQDN of your DFIR-IRIS instance.
* `DFIR-IRIS API Key`: Must contain the API Key of a user with write permissions for IOCs/Assets and read permissions for Cases.

## Installation 
The installation can be performed manually if required, either from the source code or using pre-existing packages (skip to step 3).

1. Clone this repository:  
   `git clone https://github.com/fendril/IrisWithSecureModule.git && cd IrisWithSecureModule`
2. Build the wheel package:  
   `python3 setup.py bdist_wheel` 
3. Copy the wheel into the IRIS app Docker container:  
   `docker cp iris_withsecure_module-XX-py3-none-any.whl container:/iriswebapp/dependencies/`
4. Open an interactive shell inside the container:  
   `docker exec -it container /bin/sh`
5. Install the new package:  
   `pip3 install dependencies/iris_withsecure_module-XX-py3-none-any.whl`

*Alternatively, you can use the `BUILDnPUSH2IRIS.sh` helper script available here:* * [DFIR-IRIS Quick Start Script](https://docs.dfir-iris.org/development/modules/quick_start/buildnpush2iris.sh)
* [DFIR-IRIS Processor Module Documentation](https://docs.dfir-iris.org/development/modules/quick_start/processor/#installing-and-registering-the-module)

6. Go to **Modules management** and click on **Add module**
7. Copy **iris_withsecure_module** into the form, and click **Validate module**

## Usage
When viewing a BCD within your WithSecure Elements portal, you will see an ID linked to the Category formatted as 9 digits, followed by a hyphen and 3 to 4 digits (Regex: `\d{9}-\d{3,4}`).  
**Note:** This is not the ID required by the WithSecure API. You must retrieve the actual UUID from the URL when inspecting the BCD page.  

* *Example URL:* `https://elements.withsecure.com/apps/rdrv2/broad-context-detections/45xxxx99-exxa-4xx9-axx6-58xxxxxxxxe5/summary`
* *Extracted API-compatible BCD ID:* `45xxxx99-exxa-4xx9-axx6-58xxxxxxxxe5`

Once the module is installed and configured, follow these steps within your DFIR-IRIS Case to trigger it:

1. Go to your **Case Summary**, edit it, and insert your BCD ID anywhere inside the text using a format that matches the following regex:  
   `(?i)(?:ID\s*WithSecure|ID\s*WS|WS\s*ID|WithSecure\s*ID)\s*[:=]\s*([a-z0-9]{8}(?:-[a-z0-9]{4}){3}-[a-z0-9]{12})`  
    Those exemples are valid for the module :
   - WithSecureID:45xxxx99-exxa-4xx9-axx6-58xxxxxxxxe5
   - IDWS=45xxxx99-exxa-4xx9-axx6-58xxxxxxxxe5
   - ID WithSecure :45xxxx99-exxa-4xx9-axx6-58xxxxxxxxe5
   - WSID : 45xxxx99-exxa-4xx9-axx6-58xxxxxxxxe5
   - IdWiThSeCuRe : 45xxxx99-exxa-4xx9-axx6-58xxxxxxxxe5
2. Click on the button dedicated to running manual Processors.
3. In the dropdown menu, select:  
   `iris_withsecure_module::on_manual_trigger_case`
4. After a few seconds, the Assets and IOCs extracted from the BCD ID will be populated directly into your Case.

**Note** : Actually the Module is capturing and processing one, and only one WithSecure BCD ID.
